summary: Gruber provides an update on his Perl title-casing script, originally published in May 2008. The script was ported by community members to numerous languages including JavaScript, Python, Ruby, PHP, Objective-C, and Nu. He highlights two standout contributions: David Gouch's superior JavaScript implementation that handles additional edge cases, and Aristotle Pagaltzis's clean refactoring of the original Perl script with a proper test suite. Gruber adopts Gouch's version for his own bookmarklets and credits Pagaltzis's version as better than the original in every way.
key_points: Gruber's original title-casing Perl script was ported to at least seven different programming languages by community contributors
David Gouch's JavaScript version handled more edge cases than both Gruber's original and John Resig's direct port, becoming Gruber's preferred solution for bookmarklets
Aristotle Pagaltzis refactored the Perl original into clean, logical code and added a proper test suite based on Gouch's test cases
The post illustrates how open-source sharing leads to iterative improvement, with the community producing versions superior to the original
money_quotes: It's one of those pieces of code that started small and simple, and grew ugly over time as edge cases were worked around one at a time.
I've been using this script for years, but have put off publishing it on the grounds that it looks like the sort of punctuation-riddled code that gives Perl phobics the heebie-jeebies.
The result is better than my original script in every way, and his test suite can be used to improve any implementation.
He took Gouch's test cases and turned them into a proper testing suite.
key_insight: Publishing imperfect but useful code invites community contributions that surpass the original, as demonstrated by superior ports and refactorings of Gruber's title-casing script.
